hypothesis: If a chemically informed autoencoder is used to encode periodic table representations into a structured latent space for conditional diffusion-based generation, then a framework combining domain-specific chemical priors with conditional latent diffusion will generate catalytic surface compositions that more accurately satisfy adsorption energy targets and elemental constraints compared to chemically-agnostic baselines, because encoding chemical periodicity and bonding relationships into the latent space provides a more physically meaningful generative manifold that aligns compositional diversity with catalytic performance requirements.
